name: Anda Bhurji
tagline: Spiced scrambled eggs — B12, choline, and complete protein in 10 minutes
highlight: Tomato + onion + green chili — the trinity that elevates scrambled eggs

hero.alt: Spiced scrambled eggs with onion, tomato, and coriander in a steel plate with pav
hero.caption: anda bhurji — masala scrambled eggs

healthFacts: Two eggs deliver ~12g protein, 1.6µg B12, and 290mg choline.
B12 alone covers 65% of daily need — critical for nerves and red blood cells.
Yolks contain biotin (B7), vitamin D, and selenium — keep them in.
Lecithin in yolks supports the homocysteine cycle alongside folate.

steps.title: Sauté the masala
steps.detail: Heat butter in a non-stick pan. Add onion and green chili, sauté till translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste, then tomato. Cook till tomato softens (3 min).
steps.tip: Use butter, not oil — fat-soluble vitamins from the egg yolks deserve a richer pairing.

steps.title: Pour in beaten eggs
steps.detail: Whisk eggs with salt, turmeric, and garam masala. Pour into the pan and let it sit 30 seconds without stirring, then drag a spatula through to form curds.
steps.tip: Low-medium heat — high heat makes the eggs rubbery and dries them out.

steps.title: Finish soft
steps.detail: Pull off the heat while the eggs are still glossy and slightly underdone — residual heat finishes them. Stir in coriander and serve with pav, paratha, or toast.
steps.tip: Eggs go from underdone to dry in 15 seconds — err on undercooked.
